articles.title: Economic & event calendar in Asia Wednesday, February 25, 2026: Australian inflation data
articles.snippet: The focus is the Australian CPI data for January:Australia January CPI preview: core inflation steady, electricity lifts headlineTrump will speak later. State of the Union speech. His centrepiece economic policy has completely collapsed. He's scrambling to recover:Trump considers new Section 232 tariffs after Supreme Court ruling This article was written by Eamonn Sheridan at investinglive.com.

articles.title: Australia January CPI preview: core inflation steady, electricity lifts headline
articles.snippet: January CPI is set to show electricity-driven headline strength and still-firm core inflation, keeping a May RBA hike in play.Summary:Headline CPI seen at 0.4% m/m (seasonally adjusted) in JanuaryAnnual inflation forecast at 3.6–3.7% y/yElectricity prices a key upside driver; fuel partly offsetsTrimmed mean seen at 0.3% m/m, annual pace steady at 3.3%RBA still expected to hike in May despite slight core moderationAustralia’s January CPI, due from the Australian Bureau of Statistics on Wednesday

articles.title: Australia CPI expected to point to sticky inflation, supporting hawkish stance
articles.snippet: Australia will release its key set of inflation figures for the month of January on Wednesday, with the Consumer Price Index (CPI) expected to rise by 3.7%, slightly lower than the 3.8% in the last month of 2025.

articles.title: Alumni recall rich legacy of DMC on foundation day
articles.snippet: Darbhanga Medical College celebrated its 101st Foundation Day with a series of events, including an alumni meet and academic sessions. Rajya Sabha member Sanjay Kumar Jha urged the institution to lead in emerging medical technologies like AI. Speakers lauded …

[0.67] Australia January CPI preview: core inflation steady, electricity lifts headline
January CPI is set to show electricity-driven headline strength and still-firm core inflation, keeping a May RBA hike in play.Summary:Headline CPI seen at 0.4% m/m (seasonally adjusted) in JanuaryAnnual inflation forecast at 3.6–3.7% y/yElectricity prices a key upside driver; fuel partly offsetsTrimmed mean seen at 0.3% m/m, annual pace steady at 3.3%RBA still expected to hike in May despite slight core moderationAustralia’s January CPI, due from the Australian Bureau of Statistics on Wednesday 

[0.33] Australia CPI expected to point to sticky inflation, supporting hawkish stance
Australia will release its key set of inflation figures for the month of January on Wednesday, with the Consumer Price Index (CPI) expected to rise by 3.7%, slightly lower than the 3.8% in the last month of 2025.

[0.33] Alumni recall rich legacy of DMC on foundation day
